title: Add tests for failed tool execution logging into .agent/errors.jsonl
summary: Add deterministic tests that verify failed tool executions are appended to `.agent/errors.jsonl` with the required structured fields, while ensuring the original client-facing error response remains unchanged.
acceptance_criteria: Tests verify that a failed tool execution appends one JSONL record to `.agent/errors.jsonl`.
Tests verify the error log record includes tool name, tool input, tool output/error, and timestamp.
Tests verify error logging is best-effort and does not mask or replace the original tool error returned to the client.
Tests verify error-log writes do not resolve to `/.agent/errors.jsonl` and respect the initialized project root invariant.
Updated tests contribute to keeping overall project coverage at or above 90%.

steps.description: Identify the JSON-RPC error path and the helper boundaries where failed tool execution records are appended to `.agent/errors.jsonl`.
Add tests that trigger representative tool failures and assert a structured JSONL record is written with tool name, input, output/error, and timestamp.
Add tests confirming the client still receives the original error response even if error logging is enabled.
Add negative-path assertions ensuring the error log uses the initialized project root and never falls back to `/.agent/errors.jsonl`.

notes: Keep tests hermetic and filesystem-local using temporary directories.
Prefer assertions on structured JSON content over broad text matching.
Focus on failed tool execution logging only; do not mix unrelated runtime behavior into this ticket.
